front: Work: in this novel, the ballet aficionado Shimamura has an affair with the geisha Komako at a remote hot springs town
Work: this novel ends with the protagonist feeling the Milky Way flow inside him as a fire engulfs a silk warehouse
Work: this novel opens with the protagonist watching the girl Yoko's reflection in a train window as she cares for a man with tuberculosis
Work: in this novel, Kikuji has an affair with Mrs. Ota, who commits suicide; her daughter Fumiko smashes an ancient tea bowl stained with lipstick
Work: in this novel, the hairy birthmark of Chikako disgusts the protagonist, who recalls seeing a woman trim hair from her breast
Work: in this story, a university student's infatuation with a drum-playing girl ends when he sees her bathing naked and realizes she is just an adolescent
Work: in this novel, the elderly businessman Shingo Ogata forgives his daughter-in-law Kikuko for aborting her pregnancy in protest of son Shuichi's affair
back: The Sound of the Mountain (Yasunari Kawabata)

front: Work: in this novel, the writer Oki Toshio visits his ex-lover Otoko, now a painter in a lesbian relationship with protege Keiko, years after writing A Girl of Sixteen about their affair
Work: this novel chronicles a six-month-long Go match between old master Honinbo Shusai and younger Otake, based on the author's journalism
Work: in this novella, Old Eguchi visits an establishment where impotent old men sleep next to drugged young virgins
back: The House of the Sleeping Beauties (Yasunari Kawabata)
work: The House of the Sleeping Beauties

front: Work: in this story, children play with colored lanterns and a boy's name is projected in green light on a girl's breast; a rare bell cricket is given by mistake
back: "The Grasshopper and the Bell Cricket" (Yasunari Kawabata)

front: Author: this Japanese author's Nobel lecture 'Japan, the Beautiful, and Myself' declared 'suicide is not a form of enlightenment'
Work: in this novel, the foundling Chieko discovers she has a twin sister Naeko; suitor Hideo confuses them
Work: in this humorous palm-of-the-hand story, a peasant builds a lavish outhouse but dies of back pain on his rival's pay toilet
Work: in this story, a man infatuated with the roundness of a girl's shoulder takes her talking arm home with him
